title: Developer Adoption: Risks & Next Product-Quality Improvement for Pilot Expansion
summary: Adoption signals improved after the docs refresh, but pilot expansion is gated by brittle setup/credential recovery and missing observable evidence/citations in recommendations. The next product-quality improvement should harden and publish a self-serve setup recovery path (including stale CLI and credential mismatch recovery) that can be demonstrated without support intervention.

claims.text: Quickstart completion improved from 44% to 58% after the docs copy refresh, but the biggest remaining drop is during local setup and credential configuration due to environment-specific errors.

Support volume is dominated by setup failures (environment drift, stale CLI versions, missing environment variables, and credentials from the wrong workspace), and users often need an internal recovery checklist that is not in public docs—leading some teams to re-run the quickstart and reproduce the failed state.

Pilot expansion is considered risky until failed-install/setup recovery is visibly demonstrable with documented guidance; the launch owner flagged setup recovery as the failure that would make demos feel brittle.

Pilot readiness gates explicitly block scheduling more teams until (1) public failed-install recovery steps exist and (2) generated recommendations show source citations and expose whether they came from analytics, support, or interviews.

Pilot users’ trust in generated recommendations depends on an inspectable evidence trail (source snippets/links) and clear separation of evidence types (analytics vs support vs interviews), more than prose quality.

openQuestions: Which specific recovery steps in the internal support checklist should be productized and published first (e.g., stale CLI detection/update, env var validation, workspace credential mismatch reset)?
What is the minimum in-product or lab UI needed to make recommendation citations and evidence-type provenance observable during a pilot demo?
How will success be measured for the new recovery path (e.g., reduction in setup-related tickets, reduced exits at credential setup, fewer quickstart restarts)?
recommendation: Next product-quality improvement for pilot expansion: ship and publish a self-serve setup recovery path that covers failed installs and credential/environment issues (stale CLI, missing env vars, workspace credential mismatches, and repeated setup attempts), replacing reliance on support-pasted internal checklists. Validate it in the lab as a calm, end-to-end demo flow, and in parallel ensure generated recommendations visibly include source citations and provenance (analytics/support/interviews) to meet the pilot readiness gates before scheduling the next teams.
